free_text: Measurement of Transuranium Isotopes Fission Cross
Section with Lead Neutron Slowing-Down Spectrometer
Main Ref. Table of group c-s. Figs. of point-wise c-s.
Details of spectrometer. Graphs of point-wise c-s.
Electron beam energy 50 MeV, repetition rate
275 Hz, current in pulse up to 1 A .
Carbon plate 1mm thick served as electron beam monitor.
Neutron flow was measured by Li-6(n,a)H-3 reaction.
FAKEL generate up to 10**12 neutrons in the pulse.
Samples were prepared in Radium Inst. (4RUSRI) by
the 39.93 eV resonance of 237Np(n,f) by Pearson
Pearson distribution was used in analysis to restore
A gaussian approximation of the resolution function is
absent in publication. Gaussian approximation was used
for comparison of experimental and evaluated data.)

their constancy were determined and checked during the
Time spectra of each detector were divided channel-
wise into the time spectrum of 235U(n,f).
The later was transformed into energetic ones using
the normalized fission cross section of 235U(n,f).
Error of nuclei quantities ratio for the
analyzed samples and the standard sample of U-235.

is smoothed over and its contribution is negligible in
the majority of group cross section.
